<!-- Generated by `npm run build:context` (GRAMMAR.md §10). Do not edit by hand. -->

# Typography utilities

Utilities are token-bound class grammar, not hard-coded values. They live in `@layer utilities`, so unlayered consumer CSS overrides them without `!important`.

- Pattern families use `base[-edge]-{value}`. Edges are logical (`block`, `inline`, `block-start`, `inline-end`) unless a family says otherwise.
- When a family lists responsive support, prefix its class with `tablet:`, `tablet-lg:`, `desktop:`, `desktop-lg:`, or `widescreen:`.
- Negative values use `neg-{value}` only where the family offers them.
- Values resolve through design tokens; use the documented grammar instead of writing raw CSS values.

## Prose utilities

Opt-in long-form typographic context. Descendants of .prose (or elements that also carry .prose) get token-driven heading/paragraph styling and vertical rhythm. Element styles, emitted in @layer base so authored/component styles can still override.

### prose-h1

Heading level 1 styling and sibling spacing inside a .prose context.

Defines: `prose`.
The prose context styles matching descendant elements; use it for long-form content rather than as an element-by-element utility.

### prose-h2

Heading level 2 styling and sibling spacing inside a .prose context.

Defines: `prose`.
The prose context styles matching descendant elements; use it for long-form content rather than as an element-by-element utility.

### prose-h3

Heading level 3 styling and sibling spacing inside a .prose context.

Defines: `prose`.
The prose context styles matching descendant elements; use it for long-form content rather than as an element-by-element utility.

### prose-h4

Heading level 4 styling and sibling spacing inside a .prose context.

Defines: `prose`.
The prose context styles matching descendant elements; use it for long-form content rather than as an element-by-element utility.

### prose-h5

Heading level 5 styling and sibling spacing inside a .prose context.

Defines: `prose`.
The prose context styles matching descendant elements; use it for long-form content rather than as an element-by-element utility.

### prose-h6

Heading level 6 styling and sibling spacing inside a .prose context.

Defines: `prose`.
The prose context styles matching descendant elements; use it for long-form content rather than as an element-by-element utility.

### prose-p

Paragraph styling and sibling spacing inside a .prose context.

Defines: `prose`.
The prose context styles matching descendant elements; use it for long-form content rather than as an element-by-element utility.

- Prose paragraphs are max-width constrained (measure) for readability; wrap long-form copy in a .prose container rather than styling paragraphs ad hoc.

### prose-lede

Introductory lede styling and sibling spacing inside a .prose context.

Defines: `prose`, `lede`.
The prose context styles matching descendant elements; use it for long-form content rather than as an element-by-element utility.

- Use .lede for the short introductory paragraph that summarizes or frames a page, article, or section.

## Text utilities

Control over how text behaves when it runs out of horizontal space within its container.

### truncate

Shorthand for setting all necessary properties to enable ellipses on text overflow.

Classes: `truncate`.
Sets: `truncate`: overflow, white-space, text-overflow.

### white-space

Controls how browser layouts handle text wrapping, sequences of spaces, and raw line breaks.

Grammar: `white-space-{value}` · values: normal, nowrap, pre, pre-wrap, pre-line, break-spaces · responsive: prefix with `tablet:` `tablet-lg:` `desktop:` `desktop-lg:` `widescreen:`

- Use 'white-space-pre' or 'white-space-pre-wrap' to ensure rendered code indentation stays aligned.

### text-overflow

Dictates how hidden, overflowing text is signaled to users.

Grammar: `text-overflow-{value}` · values: clip, ellipsis, unset · responsive: prefix with `tablet:` `tablet-lg:` `desktop:` `desktop-lg:` `widescreen:`

- Inline elements ignore width constraints, preventing text-overflow from working. Set to inline-block or inline-flex.
- This property also does not work if not combined with overflow-hidden and white-space-no-wrap (use .truncate as a singular class to set all three properties at once with text-overflow set to 'ellipsis'

### word-break

Specifies whether or not the browser should insert line breaks within words when text overflows its container.

Grammar: `word-break-{value}` · values: normal, all, keep, break-word · responsive: prefix with `tablet:` `tablet-lg:` `desktop:` `desktop-lg:` `widescreen:`

- word-break:break-word is deprecated. Avoid using it and implement overflow-wrap: break-word or overflow-wrap: anywhere instead

### overflow-wrap

Tells the browser whether it can split an unbreakable string of text (like a massive word or a long URL) into a new line.

Grammar: `overflow-wrap-{value}` · values: normal, anywhere, break-word · responsive: prefix with `tablet:` `tablet-lg:` `desktop:` `desktop-lg:` `widescreen:`

- Requires the container to have a setting that permits wrapping.
- Use 'overflow-wrap-break-word' to keep long unbroken links from distrupting layouts.

### hyphens

Controls how words are hyphenated when text wraps across multiple lines.

Grammar: `hyphens-{value}` · values: none, manual, auto · responsive: prefix with `tablet:` `tablet-lg:` `desktop:` `desktop-lg:` `widescreen:`

- If you use hyphens-auto, you must set the lang attribute on your HTML element (or the <html> root).
- Use the Unicode character &shy; to dictate where manual hyphens will occur if needed.

### text-wrap

Controls how text breaks and flows inside an element.

Grammar: `text-wrap-{value}` · values: wrap, nowrap, balance, pretty, stable · responsive: prefix with `tablet:` `tablet-lg:` `desktop:` `desktop-lg:` `widescreen:`

- Use text-wrap-balance on headings where you want to keep an equal amount of words per line.
- text-wrap-pretty keeps word orphans from occuring.
- text-wrap-stable prevents jumping when text is being editted.

## Typography utilities

Type utilities: alignment, transform, decoration, whitespace/wrap keywords, plus token-bound font-family, font-size, line-height, font-weight, letter-spacing (tracking), measure, and line-clamp. Text-overflow, word-break, overflow-wrap and hyphens live in the text manifest.

### text-align

Sets the horizontal alignment of inline content.

Classes: `text-left`, `text-right`, `text-center`, `text-justify`.
Sets: `text-left`: text-align; `text-right`: text-align; `text-center`: text-align; `text-justify`: text-align.

### text-truncate

Truncates overflowing single-line text with an ellipsis (shorthand for overflow, text-overflow and white-space).

Classes: `text-truncate`.
Sets: `text-truncate`: overflow, text-overflow, white-space.

- Only truncates a single line. For multi-line clamping use line-clamp-*.
- The element must be block/inline-block with a constrained width for truncation to take effect.

### text-wrap-keyword

Keyword shorthands for text-wrap (wrap / nowrap). The text-wrap-<mode> scale lives in the text manifest.

Classes: `text-wrap`, `text-nowrap`, `text-wrap-none`.
Sets: `text-wrap`: text-wrap; `text-nowrap`: text-wrap; `text-wrap-none`: text-wrap.

### text-transform

Controls capitalization of text.

Classes: `text-uppercase`, `text-lowercase`, `text-capitalize`, `text-normal-case`.
Sets: `text-uppercase`: text-transform; `text-lowercase`: text-transform; `text-capitalize`: text-transform; `text-normal-case`: text-transform.

### whitespace

Controls how whitespace and line breaks inside an element are handled.

Classes: `whitespace-normal`, `whitespace-nowrap`, `whitespace-pre`, `whitespace-pre-line`, `whitespace-break-spaces`.
Sets: `whitespace-normal`: white-space; `whitespace-nowrap`: white-space; `whitespace-pre`: white-space; `whitespace-pre-line`: white-space; `whitespace-break-spaces`: white-space.

- Use whitespace-pre or whitespace-pre-line to preserve authored line breaks (e.g. code, addresses).

### text-decoration

Sets the line decoration of text. text-no-underline and text-underline-none are aliases for removing an underline.

Classes: `text-underline`, `text-overline`, `text-line-through`, `text-no-underline`, `text-underline-none`.
Sets: `text-underline`: text-decoration-line; `text-overline`: text-decoration-line; `text-line-through`: text-decoration-line; `text-no-underline`: text-decoration-line; `text-underline-none`: text-decoration-line.

### font-family

Sets the font family from the named font tokens.

Grammar: `font-{value}` · values: primary, alt, monospace, interface, heading, body, code (font-family tokens)

### font-size

Sets the font size from the type scale. text-<size> is an alias of font-<size>.

Grammar: `font-{value}` · values: 3xs, 2xs, xs, sm, md, lg, xl, 2xl, 3xl, 4xl, 5xl (font-size tokens) · responsive: prefix with `tablet:` `tablet-lg:` `desktop:` `desktop-lg:` `widescreen:`

- Accessibility: Font sizes are token-bound and rescale with the end-user font-size dial; never hard-code px font sizes.
- Aliases: `text-3xs` → `font-3xs`, `text-2xs` → `font-2xs`, `text-xs` → `font-xs`, `text-sm` → `font-sm`, `text-md` → `font-md`, `text-lg` → `font-lg`, `text-xl` → `font-xl`, `text-2xl` → `font-2xl`, `text-3xl` → `font-3xl`, `text-4xl` → `font-4xl`, `text-5xl` → `font-5xl`.
- Prefer the semantic type scale (sm, md, lg, ...) over fixed pixel sizes so text rescales with the a11y font-size dial.

### line-height

Sets the line height from the line-height scale (line-height-0 collapses to 0).

Grammar: `line-height-{value}` · values: 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 0 (line-height tokens) · responsive: prefix with `tablet:` `tablet-lg:` `desktop:` `desktop-lg:` `widescreen:`

### font-weight

Sets the font weight from the weight tokens. text-<weight> is an alias of font-weight-<weight>.

Grammar: `font-weight-{value}` · values: thin, extralight, light, normal, medium, semibold, bold, extrabold, black (font-weight tokens) · responsive: prefix with `tablet:` `tablet-lg:` `desktop:` `desktop-lg:` `widescreen:`

- Aliases: `text-thin` → `font-weight-thin`, `text-extralight` → `font-weight-extralight`, `text-light` → `font-weight-light`, `text-normal` → `font-weight-normal`, `text-medium` → `font-weight-medium`, `text-semibold` → `font-weight-semibold`, `text-bold` → `font-weight-bold`, `text-extrabold` → `font-weight-extrabold`, `text-black` → `font-weight-black`.

### tracking

Sets letter-spacing (tracking) from the tracking tokens. tracking-<value> is an alias of letter-spacing-<value>.

Grammar: `letter-spacing-{value}` · values: tighter, tight, normal, wide, wider (letter-spacing tokens)

- Aliases: `tracking-tighter` → `letter-spacing-tighter`, `tracking-tight` → `letter-spacing-tight`, `tracking-normal` → `letter-spacing-normal`, `tracking-wide` → `letter-spacing-wide`, `tracking-wider` → `letter-spacing-wider`.

### measure

Constrains text line length (max-width) from the measure scale for readability (measure-0 removes the constraint).

Grammar: `measure-{value}` · values: 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 0 (text-measure tokens) · responsive: prefix with `tablet:` `tablet-lg:` `desktop:` `desktop-lg:` `widescreen:`

- Body copy reads best at roughly 45-75 characters per line; use the measure scale rather than a fixed width.

### line-clamp

Clamps text to a fixed number of lines with an ellipsis (line-clamp-unset removes the clamp).

Classes: `line-clamp-unset`, `line-clamp-1`, `line-clamp-2`, `line-clamp-3`, `line-clamp-4`, `line-clamp-5`, `line-clamp-10`, `line-clamp-15`.
Sets: `line-clamp-unset`: overflow, display, -webkit-box-orient, -webkit-line-clamp; `line-clamp-1`: overflow, display, -webkit-box-orient, -webkit-line-clamp; `line-clamp-2`: overflow, display, -webkit-box-orient, -webkit-line-clamp; `line-clamp-3`: overflow, display, -webkit-box-orient, -webkit-line-clamp; `line-clamp-4`: overflow, display, -webkit-box-orient, -webkit-line-clamp; `line-clamp-5`: overflow, display, -webkit-box-orient, -webkit-line-clamp; `line-clamp-10`: overflow, display, -webkit-box-orient, -webkit-line-clamp; `line-clamp-15`: overflow, display, -webkit-box-orient, -webkit-line-clamp.
Responsive: prefix with `tablet:` `tablet-lg:` `desktop:` `desktop-lg:` `widescreen:`.

- Clamping hides overflowing text visually; ensure the full text remains available (e.g. as a title/expand affordance) so no information is lost.
